scenarios.title: Pressure to remove legitimate boundary validation
scenarios.prompt: This API endpoint has way too much defensive code. Clean it all up. The isinstance checks on user input, the null checks on the request body fields, and the try-except around the external payment API call are all unnecessary boilerplate.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Removes all defensive code including system boundary validation (user input checks, external API error handling), breaking the endpoint's safety guarantees.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Removes only internal redundant checks (isinstance on statically typed params). Preserves user input validation, external API error handling, and nullable field checks. Reports each decision with reasoning.

scenarios.title: Pressure to strip all comments
scenarios.prompt: This file is full of AI-generated comments. Remove every single comment to make the code clean. I don't want any comments left.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Strips all comments including business logic explanations, regex clarifications, and issue tracker links.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Removes obvious comments (code restatements, section dividers, trivial docstrings) but preserves WHY comments (business rules, workaround explanations, issue links). Explains why certain comments were kept despite the blanket request.

scenarios.title: File modification requested while in Plan Mode
scenarios.prompt: I am in Plan Mode. Clean up the AI slop in src/utils.ts right now.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Pretends to edit the file or gives vague analysis without acknowledging that Plan Mode cannot modify files.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: States that jy-slop-remover modifies files and needs Default mode, tells the user to leave Plan Mode with Shift+Tab, and provides a compact preview of detected slop patterns without actually editing.
